_metadata.schema: CS Operations AI Assistant — 25-Key Knowledge Base
_metadata.description: Comprehensive CS knowledge base for Chuck AI extension. Covers product architecture, health model, dashboard navigation, margin/COGS, credit lifecycle, engagement workflows, bureau optimization, guardrails, and cross-sell. Supports 4 CS modes: health_check, reprice_scenario, utilization_guidance, disposition_response.

product_architecture.description: System overview, module relationships, integration points
source_files: Total Solutions Use Case Process Flow.pdf

content.platform_overview: Informativ is a SaaS compliance and automotive dealership technology platform providing an end-to-end solution for credit, compliance, fraud prevention, and payment desking. Core tagline: 'Smarter Deals. Safer Sales.'

value_framework.name: Make More / Save More / Stay Safe

drivers.make_more: Increase close rate and lead quality. Soft pull pre-qualification, SmartPencil deal desking, Customer Insights dashboard, QR code lead capture, eZ Form Fill, website credit applications.
drivers.save_more: Reduce operating costs. Soft pull strategy (replace hard pulls), Credit Guardrails, fraud prevention suite, Verified STIPs recontract reduction, flat-rate predictable pricing.
drivers.stay_safe: Minimize regulatory, legal, and audit risk. Compliance Platform (Red Flags, OFAC, MLA, Adverse Action), digital deal jackets, synthetic ID detection, eVerify DL scan, SIM validation, FTC Safeguards enforcement.

core_modules.credit: Hard pull and soft pull credit reports through Equifax, Experian, TransUnion. Integrates with RouteOne, DealerTrack, CUDL, and major DMS platforms.
core_modules.compliance: FTC Safeguards Rule enforcement, Red Flags Hard Stop, OFAC screening, Adverse Action Letter Fulfillment, digital deal jackets with secure 5-year storage, enforceable compliant workflows.
core_modules.fraud_prevention: DL scanning with DMV data comparison, document authenticity check, LiveID, SIM validation, synthetic ID detection, multi-layer fraud alerts.
core_modules.smartpencil_payments: Built-in real-time payment calculator. 275+ banks, lender programs, and regional incentives. Zip-code level TTL accuracy. Credit-qualified lease and loan payments. Turns every desk manager into an A-player.
core_modules.turbopass: Income and identity verification suite. Report types: Bravo (banking 120-day), Alpha (banking 90-day), Paystub (payroll), Insurance, Zulu (utility), Identity (DL/passport/state ID with DMV comparison).
core_modules.customer_insights: Dashboard providing customer-level analytics, health scoring, and behavioral insights for the sales floor.
core_modules.credit_guardrails: Showroom credit spend controls — alerts when pulling patterns exceed thresholds, preventing unnecessary bureau costs.

what_informativ_is_not: Not a CRM system (integrates with Salesforce/CRMs)
Not a standalone desking tool (SmartPencil complements existing desking systems)

pricing_tiers.description: Tier definitions, feature inclusions, upgrade/downgrade rules

content.naming_evolution: Informativ has transitioned from a legacy credit-first tier system (Foundation/Detect/Protect/Control) to a SaaS-first tier architecture (Protect/Enrich/Elevate/Control). The new system anchors every customer on the compliance platform first, then layers features. Foundation and Detect are retired as standalone credit-first tiers.

protect.includes: Compliance Platform, Fraud Prevention, Hard Pull Credit (5 included)
protect.best_for: Dealers prioritizing compliance control and fraud prevention as baseline
protect.value_message: Stop risk before it costs you. Stay protected, stay compliant, stay in business.
key_features: Digital deal jacket creation and secure 5-year storage
Enforceable compliant workflows with process stops and real-time notifications
Fraud detection in showroom and remote environments
Integration with RouteOne, DealerTrack, DMS, and inventory tools

enrich.tagline: Everything in Protect + SmartPencil Deal Desking
enrich.includes: All of Protect + SmartPencil (instant payment calculations, F&I desk integration)
enrich.best_for: Dealers wanting compliance + faster deal desking and improved PVR at F&I desk
enrich.value_message: Compliant deals, faster. Protect the dealership and profit the desk.
enrich.note: Customer credit score is NOT visible to dealer (proprietary backend scoring)

elevate.tagline: Everything in Enrich + Customer Insights + Credit Spend Controls
elevate.includes: All of Enrich + Customer Insights, Soft Pull (10 included), Credit Guardrails
elevate.best_for: Dealers controlling credit spend, using soft pulls to pre-qualify, maximizing lead conversion
elevate.value_message: Sell smarter. Know your buyer before the desk. Control your costs while you do it.
elevate.note: Includes SmartPencil with full soft-pull score visibility

control.tagline: Full Platform: Hard + Soft Credit, Complete Fraud + Compliance + Insights
control.includes: Everything in Elevate + Hard Pull Credit (10 included)
control.best_for: Multi-rooftop groups and high-volume dealers requiring full-platform control across all locations
control.value_message: Complete visibility. Complete control. Fraud stopped. Deals funded. Compliance locked.

foundation.tagline: Credit Reports Wherever You Need Them
foundation.reason: Low margin, no compliance or fraud value, trained market to see Informativ as commodity credit vendor

detect.tagline: Credit Reports with Essential Compliance & Fraud Protection
detect.reason: Insufficient differentiation from Foundation; compliance value was fragmented

also_referenced.streamline: Compliance & sales automation for dealers with disconnected/paper-heavy workflows
also_referenced.accelerate: Multi-rooftop control, funding speed; for enterprise groups and high-volume rooftops

upgrade_triggers: Customer outgrows current tier features (e.g., needs SmartPencil, soft pulls, or multi-rooftop dashboard)
Compliance gaps identified during health review or audit
Bureau cost optimization opportunity (soft pull strategy reduces hard pull spend)
Cross-sell signal from CSA identifying utilization of features in next tier
Margin erosion requiring SmartPencil or guardrail controls
downgrade_considerations: Customer explicitly requests feature reduction (rare — usually a retention risk signal)
Customer consolidating rooftops and no longer needs enterprise features
Always escalate downgrade requests to CS Manager (Kiristen) before processing

bureau_economics.description: Bureau sources, cost models, revenue impact

content.bureau_overview: Informativ pulls credit reports from three major bureaus: Equifax, Experian, and TransUnion. Each bureau has different cost rates for hard pulls vs. soft pulls. Bureau costs are a primary COGS driver and directly impact customer margin.

bureau_sources.equifax: Full credit reports, fraud alerts, OFAC screening integration
bureau_sources.experian: Full credit reports, soft pull pre-qualification, Vantage score support
bureau_sources.transunion: Full credit reports, identity verification, SIM validation data

pull_types.hard_pull: Full credit inquiry that impacts customer credit score. Used for deal finalization. Higher bureau cost. Required for lender funding decisions.
pull_types.soft_pull: Credit inquiry that does NOT impact customer score. Used for pre-qualification and SmartPencil payment scenarios. Lower bureau cost. Key strategy for reducing overall credit spend.

cost_model.structure: Bureau costs are charged per-pull to Informativ, then factored into customer pricing through tiered subscription rates. Hard pulls cost more than soft pulls. 3-bureau pulls cost 3x single-bureau pulls.
cost_drivers: Number of bureaus pulled per transaction (1-bureau vs. 3-bureau)
cost_model.margin_impact: Bureau costs are the largest variable COGS component. Customers who over-pull (too many hard pulls, 3-bureau when 1 would suffice) erode margin. Soft pull strategy is the primary lever for margin improvement.

revenue_impact.pricing_philosophy: Flat-rate subscription pricing absorbs bureau cost variability. This creates predictable revenue for Informativ but means high-volume pullers can become negative-margin accounts.
margin_protection_levers: Soft pull adoption (reduces hard pull volume)
SmartPull configuration (smart routing to lowest-cost bureau)
Tier upgrades that include more pulls in the base price

content.overview: The Customer Health Score is a composite metric calculated across 5 dimensions, each weighted to reflect its importance to customer retention and revenue health. The model uses a two-track system: one set of thresholds for credit/tiered customers (CSM-managed) and another for flat-rate customers (CSA-managed).

scoring_model.calculation: Each dimension scores 0-100, then is multiplied by its weight. Final score = sum of weighted dimension scores.

credit_tiered_track.customer_type: Credit-tier customers with variable bureau usage
credit_tiered_track.key_focus: Margin protection, bureau cost optimization, reprice scenarios

content.velocity_interpretation: Health score direction matters as much as absolute value. A customer at 65 trending down is more concerning than a customer at 55 trending up. Velocity is calculated as the 3-month rolling change in health score.

health_dimensions.description: 5 dimensions explained, color coding, interpretation rules

revenue_trend.measures: Month-over-month and quarter-over-quarter revenue trajectory. Includes MRR, ARR, and expansion/contraction signals.
revenue_trend.green: Revenue stable or growing. No contraction signals.
revenue_trend.amber: Revenue flat or showing early decline (1-2 months). Requires monitoring.
revenue_trend.red: Revenue declining 3+ months or significant single-month drop (>15%). Immediate attention required.

margin_health.measures: Gross margin percentage after bureau COGS. Two-track: credit customers target 27%+ GM, flat-rate customers measured against fixed cost structure.
margin_health.green: Margin above target threshold. Bureau costs well-managed.
margin_health.amber: Margin within 5 points of floor. Bureau cost creep or pricing misalignment detected.
margin_health.red: Margin below floor or negative. Immediate reprice or bureau optimization needed.

utilization.measures: Actual usage vs. allotted capacity. For credit customers: pull volume vs. subscription allotment. For flat-rate: feature engagement and product usage breadth.
utilization.amber: Under-utilization (<40% — churn risk) or over-utilization (>90% — margin risk).
utilization.red: Severe under-utilization (<20% — high churn risk) or severe over-utilization (>110% — negative margin).

engagement.measures: Login frequency, support ticket patterns, NPS response, communication responsiveness, feature adoption breadth.
engagement.green: Regular logins, proactive support engagement, positive NPS.
engagement.amber: Declining logins, increasing support tickets, no NPS response.
engagement.red: No logins 30+ days, escalated support patterns, negative NPS.

bureau_efficiency.measures: Ratio of productive pulls (leading to funded deals) vs. total pulls. Bureau mix efficiency (1-bureau vs. 3-bureau usage patterns).
bureau_efficiency.green: Efficient pull patterns. Soft pull adoption where available. Reasonable bureau mix.
bureau_efficiency.amber: Excessive 3-bureau pulling. Low soft pull adoption. Bureau costs trending up.
bureau_efficiency.red: Wasteful pull patterns. 3-bureau on every transaction. No soft pull usage despite availability.

amber.action: Monthly review. Proactive outreach. Address declining dimensions.

red.action: Weekly monitoring. Escalation to CS Manager. Retention intervention.

score_interpretation.description: Red/amber/green thresholds, action triggers, escalation rules

actions: Review tier fit — is upgrade warranted?
green_70_plus.csa_focus: Feature deepening, training refresher, renewal prep

actions: Schedule health review call with customer
amber_40_69.csm_focus: Margin recovery, reprice evaluation, bureau optimization
amber_40_69.csa_focus: Utilization improvement, feature re-training, engagement plan

actions: Create retention plan with specific remediation steps
Document all actions in CI Dashboard disposition
red_below_40.csm_focus: Immediate reprice or margin recovery, escalation to leadership if contract at risk
red_below_40.csa_focus: Emergency re-engagement, on-site visit if warranted, product team escalation for adoption blockers

escalation_rules: Any customer dropping 15+ points in a single month → automatic escalation to CS Manager
Any customer with health score <30 for 2+ consecutive months → escalation to Director of Client Operations
Any Tier I customer (≥$100K ARR) entering amber → immediate CS Manager review
Negative margin customer → escalation to CS Manager + CRO notification
NPS detractor (score 0-6) → immediate outreach within 24 hours

velocity_alerts.rapid_decline: Health score dropping >10 points/month for 2+ months. Trigger: immediate review regardless of absolute score.
velocity_alerts.recovery_signal: Health score rising >5 points/month for 2+ months after intervention. Trigger: document what worked, reduce monitoring cadence.
velocity_alerts.stagnation: Health score flat (±2 points) for 3+ months in amber zone. Trigger: escalate — current interventions are not working.

margin_thresholds.description: Two-track thresholds (tiered vs. credit), decline indicators

credit_tiered_customers.description: Credit-tier customers have variable COGS driven by bureau pull volume and mix. Margin must be actively managed through reprice conversations and bureau optimization.

flat_rate_customers.margin_model: Fixed subscription revenue against fixed COGS. Margin is more predictable but can erode if product costs increase or customer receives discounted pricing.
warning_signals: Customer locked into legacy pricing significantly below current rates
Product costs increasing without price adjustment

decline_indicators: Negative margin (below 0% — escalation to CS Manager + CRO)
Bureau COGS growing faster than revenue for 2+ months
Customer's pull volume increasing without corresponding revenue increase

response_by_severity.watch_20_27_percent: Monitor monthly. Prepare reprice scenario. Identify bureau optimization opportunities.
response_by_severity.warning_10_20_percent: Proactive outreach. Schedule reprice conversation. Build bureau mix analysis. Escalate to CS Manager.
response_by_severity.critical_below_10_percent: Immediate escalation. Reprice required. Bureau optimization mandatory. CS Manager leads conversation with customer.
response_by_severity.negative_below_0_percent: Emergency protocol. CS Manager + CRO involvement. NMA (Negative Margin Analysis) freeze protocol. Contract review.

dashboard_navigation.description: Portfolio Overview, Health Table, Tier Analysis, Guardrails tabs

content.overview: The Customer Intelligence (CI) Dashboard is a full-portfolio customer health platform deployed at customer-intelligence-dashboard.netlify.app. It provides Account Managers and CS team members with real-time health scoring, revenue tiering, NMA flags, bureau intelligence, guardrail predictions, cross-sell scoring, and disposition tracking.

primary_tabs.portfolio_overview: Landing page showing KPI summary cards (total customers, total MRR, average health score, tier distribution), portfolio health distribution chart, and top-level metrics.
primary_tabs.customer_health: Sortable/filterable table of all customers with health scores, revenue, margin, utilization, and engagement metrics. Click any row to open the detail panel.
primary_tabs.tier_analysis: Breakdown of customers by revenue tier (Tier I: ≥$100K ARR, Tier II: $20K-$99K, Tier III: <$20K). Shows tier-level aggregate health, revenue, and margin metrics.
primary_tabs.cross_sell: Identifies expansion opportunities based on current product usage, tier position, and cross-sell scoring model.
primary_tabs.bureau_intelligence: Bureau mix analysis, cost trends, soft pull adoption rates, and bureau efficiency metrics per customer.
primary_tabs.guardrails: CPQ allotment tracking, utilization vs. guardrail thresholds, overage predictions, and proactive alert indicators.
primary_tabs.disposition_tracking: Logs all CS team dispositions (actions taken on customer accounts), tracks follow-up status, and provides audit trail.
primary_tabs.my_customers: Filtered view showing only the logged-in user's assigned accounts with their health and action items.
primary_tabs.cs_management: Manager-level view with team performance, disposition completion rates, and portfolio-level trends.
primary_tabs.cpq_alerts: CPQ-specific alerts showing customers approaching or exceeding their quoted allotments, with recommended actions.

daily_workflow.morning_check: 1. Open Portfolio Overview for KPI snapshot. 2. Switch to Customer Health tab, sort by health score ascending to see worst-performing accounts first. 3. Check Disposition Tracking for pending follow-ups.
daily_workflow.action_items: 4. Open detail panel for any red/amber customers. 5. Review T12 financials and bureau history. 6. Log dispositions for any actions taken.
daily_workflow.weekly_review: 7. Check Cross-Sell tab for expansion opportunities. 8. Review Bureau Intelligence for optimization candidates. 9. Review Guardrails for overage risks.

tab_reference.description: Per-tab purpose, key workflows, filter patterns

portfolio_overview.purpose: Executive summary of portfolio health and key performance indicators

customer_health.purpose: Detailed customer-level health data with drill-down capability
key_workflows: Detail panel access for T12 financials
customer_health.detail_panel: Click any customer row to open detail panel showing T12 revenue history, T12 bureau history, margin trends, disposition history, and reprice calculator.

cross_sell.purpose: Identify and prioritize expansion opportunities across the portfolio

bureau_intelligence.purpose: Bureau cost analysis and optimization opportunity identification

guardrails.purpose: CPQ allotment monitoring and overage prevention
guardrails.guardrail_labels: Guardrail abbreviations appear as tooltips in the dashboard. Common labels include credit pull limits, bureau allotments, and product usage caps.

disposition_tracking.purpose: Track all CS team actions and ensure follow-through

cpq_alerts.purpose: CPQ-specific monitoring for customers approaching quoted allotment limits
cpq_alerts.quarter_fallback: On the first day of a new quarter, CPQ tab automatically falls back to the most recent complete quarter data and shows an amber banner. No manual intervention needed.

filter_workflows.description: Common filter combinations, sort orders, export patterns

daily_triage.filters: CS Owner = [Your Name], Health Status = Red + Amber, Sort by Health Score ascending
daily_triage.use_case: Morning workflow — identify accounts needing immediate attention

margin_recovery.filters: CS Owner = [Your Name], Margin < 20%, Sort by Margin ascending
margin_recovery.use_case: CSM reprice preparation — identify margin-eroding accounts

bureau_optimization.description: Find accounts with bureau cost opportunities
bureau_optimization.filters: Bureau Mix = 3-Bureau, Soft Pull Adoption = None, Sort by Bureau Cost descending
bureau_optimization.use_case: Bureau optimization campaign — identify accounts pulling too many bureaus

expansion_pipeline.filters: Health Status = Green, Cross-Sell Score > 70, Sort by Cross-Sell Score descending
expansion_pipeline.use_case: Weekly expansion review — build upsell pipeline

tier_review.description: Analyze tier distribution and health by tier
tier_review.filters: Tier = [Specific Tier], Sort by Revenue descending
tier_review.use_case: Tier analysis — understand health patterns within each revenue tier

pending_dispositions.filters: CS Owner = [Your Name], Disposition Status = Open, Sort by Date ascending
pending_dispositions.use_case: Follow-up management — ensure all dispositions are completed

sort_best_practices: Health Score ascending = worst-first triage (daily workflow)
Margin ascending = worst-margin accounts first (reprice prep)
Cross-Sell Score descending = best expansion opportunities first
export_patterns: Customer Health tab supports export to CSV for offline analysis
Disposition Tracking can be exported for management reporting
Bureau Intelligence data can be exported for cost analysis

bureau_costs.description: Largest variable COGS component. Cost of credit bureau pulls (Equifax, Experian, TransUnion) charged per-pull to Informativ.
drivers: Pull volume (number of pulls per month)
bureau_costs.percentage_of_cogs: Typically 50-70% of total COGS for credit-tier customers

product_costs.description: Costs associated with product delivery infrastructure, third-party data feeds, and processing.
components: TurboPass verification costs (banking, payroll, insurance, identity reports)
Fraud detection data feeds (SIM validation, DMV data)

overhead_allocation.description: Allocated infrastructure and support costs per customer.

content.margin_calculation: Gross Margin = (Revenue - Total COGS) / Revenue × 100. Target: 27%+ for credit-tier, varies for flat-rate.
cogs_optimization_levers: Bureau volume negotiation (aggregate Informativ volume for better per-pull rates)
Soft pull adoption (soft pulls cost significantly less than hard pulls)
Bureau mix optimization (1-bureau where appropriate instead of 3-bureau)
SmartPull routing (route to lowest-cost bureau when multiple are equivalent)
Product bundling (spread fixed product costs across more features)

rate_structure.overview: Bureau costs vary by bureau, pull type, and volume tier. Rates are negotiated at the Informativ corporate level and applied across all customer accounts.

hard_pull_rates.description: Standard hard credit pull rates by bureau. These represent the cost TO Informativ per pull.
hard_pull_rates.note: Exact dollar rates are maintained in cogs-rates.json and updated quarterly through Snowflake refresh. CSM/CSA should reference CI Dashboard for current rates rather than memorizing specific dollar amounts.
hard_pull_rates.relative_cost: Hard pulls cost 3-5x more than soft pulls depending on bureau

soft_pull_rates.description: Soft pull rates are significantly lower than hard pulls. This is the primary cost lever for margin improvement.
soft_pull_rates.adoption_impact: Converting a customer from 100% hard pulls to 50% soft pull / 50% hard pull can improve margin by 5-10 percentage points

multi_bureau_impact.1_bureau: Single bureau pull — lowest cost option. Appropriate for many deal scenarios.
multi_bureau_impact.2_bureau: Two bureau pulls — moderate cost. Used when lender requires multiple bureau validation.
multi_bureau_impact.3_bureau: Three bureau pulls — highest cost. Often used habitually rather than by necessity. Primary optimization target.

cost_conversation_framework.csm_context: When reviewing bureau costs with a credit-tier customer, focus on total monthly bureau spend, pull mix (hard vs. soft), and bureau count patterns. Use CI Dashboard Bureau Intelligence tab for data.
cost_conversation_framework.csa_context: For flat-rate customers, bureau costs are less of a direct conversation topic but affect Informativ's margin on the account. CSAs should flag accounts where bureau usage patterns suggest the customer may benefit from a tier upgrade.

gross_margin_percent.interpretation: Higher = more profitable. Below 27% signals need for reprice or COGS reduction.

gross_margin_dollars.use_case: Absolute dollar margin matters for prioritization — a $500/mo customer at 20% margin ($100 margin) is less impactful than a $5000/mo customer at 25% margin ($1250 margin).

blended_margin.formula: Total Portfolio Revenue - Total Portfolio COGS / Total Portfolio Revenue × 100
blended_margin.use_case: CS Manager uses blended margin to evaluate overall portfolio health and identify systemic pricing or cost issues.

margin_components_in_dashboard.revenue_line: Monthly recurring revenue from customer subscription + any usage-based charges
margin_components_in_dashboard.cogs_line: Bureau costs + product costs + allocated overhead
margin_components_in_dashboard.margin_line: Revenue minus COGS (shown as both % and $)
margin_components_in_dashboard.t12_trend: Trailing 12-month margin trend showing whether margin is improving, stable, or declining

reprice_math.description: When considering a reprice, the calculation determines what price increase is needed to restore margin to target.
reprice_math.formula: Required Revenue = Current COGS / (1 - Target Margin%). Price Increase = Required Revenue - Current Revenue.
reprice_math.example: Customer COGS = $3000/mo, Current Revenue = $3500/mo (14% margin). To reach 27% margin: Required Revenue = $3000 / 0.73 = $4110. Price increase needed: $610/mo.
guardrails: Never propose a price increase >25% in a single conversation without CS Manager approval
Always pair price increase with value justification (new features, improved service, market rate comparison)
Tier migration may be more palatable than a raw price increase (adding features justifies higher price)

1_closed_won.description: Sales team closes the deal. Implementation handoff begins.
1_closed_won.cs_role: CSA receives handoff from RSM. Attends Implementation Kick-Off call. Establishes relationship with key dealer contacts.
1_closed_won.risk: DNM (Did Not Materialize) — customer never completes implementation. Managed by DNM SOP with CE3 engagement process.

2_implementation.description: System setup, configuration, training, and go-live. Target: first value within 14 days.
2_implementation.cs_role: CSA drives implementation timeline. Ensures dealer staff are trained. Monitors for blockers.
2_implementation.risk: Slow implementation → disengagement → DNM risk. Track closely with Implementation Team.

3_activation.description: Customer is live and actively using the platform. First 90 days are critical adoption window.
3_activation.cs_role: CSA monitors utilization patterns. Ensures adoption breadth (not just credit pulls, but compliance features, fraud tools, etc.).
3_activation.risk: Under-utilization in first 90 days is strongest predictor of churn within 12 months.

4_stabilization.description: Customer settles into regular usage patterns. Health score stabilizes. Margin patterns become clear.
4_stabilization.cs_role: CSM takes over for credit-tier accounts (margin management, reprice readiness). CSA continues flat-rate accounts (feature deepening, utilization optimization).
4_stabilization.risk: Margin erosion begins showing. Bureau cost patterns become evident.

5_steady_state.description: Mature customer relationship. Focus shifts to retention, expansion, and margin optimization.
5_steady_state.cs_role: CSM: margin protection, reprice conversations, bureau optimization. CSA: feature adoption, cross-sell identification, renewal prep.
5_steady_state.risk: Complacency — health score can decline slowly without proactive monitoring.

6_renewal_or_churn.description: Contract renewal decision point. Data-driven renewal conversation using health score, utilization, and ROI data.
6_renewal_or_churn.cs_role: CSA prepares renewal package with utilization data and ROI proof. CSM addresses any margin or pricing concerns.
key_milestones: Renewal notice sent (90 days before expiry)
6_renewal_or_churn.risk: Competitor displacement, unresolved pricing concerns, feature dissatisfaction. Earliest warning: declining engagement 6+ months before renewal.

risk_inflection_points: Day 0-14: Implementation velocity — slow starts predict DNM
Day 14-90: Adoption breadth — single-feature users churn at 3x rate
Month 3-6: Margin clarity — bureau cost patterns reveal pricing misalignment
Month 6-9: Engagement plateau — declining logins predict renewal risk
Month 9-12: Renewal preparation — late prep = reactive negotiation

retention_triggers.description: Health score declines, margin erosion, usage patterns

high_risk: Health score below 30 for 2+ consecutive months
Support tickets escalated 3+ times in 60 days
moderate_risk: Health score decline of 15+ points in single month
Requests for feature they already have (training gap signal)
early_warning: Engagement score trending down while other dimensions stable
Request for pricing breakdown or competitor comparison
Key stakeholder change (new GM, new CFO)

margin_erosion_triggers: Bureau COGS growing faster than revenue
Customer pull volume increasing without revenue increase
Legacy pricing significantly below current rate card

immediate_actions: Schedule personal call within 48 hours of trigger
Review CI Dashboard detail panel for root cause
Prepare specific talking points based on trigger type
short_term_plan: Involve CS Manager if Tier I account or negative margin
retention_playbook.escalation_path: CSA/CSM → CS Manager (Kiristen) → Director of Client Operations → CRO (Michael) for critical accounts

credit_dispositions.description: Disposition types mapped to CSM/CSA actions

recommended_actions: Document current health score and trends
Note any customer concerns or requests

retention_intervention.role: Both (CSM for credit-tier, CSA for flat-rate)
recommended_actions: Document risk signals and root cause
Escalate to CS Manager if Tier I

expansion.role: CSA identifies, CSM closes (credit-tier) or CSA closes (flat-rate)

recommended_actions: Create support ticket if not already exists

nps_response.urgency: Detractor = Immediate, Passive = Standard, Promoter = Low

recommended_actions.promoter: Thank customer, request testimonial/referral, log positive feedback
recommended_actions.passive: Identify improvement areas, schedule call to discuss experience
recommended_actions.detractor: Immediate personal outreach within 24 hours, root cause analysis, remediation plan

content.disposition_workflow: 1. Open CI Dashboard → Disposition Tracking tab. 2. Select customer. 3. Choose disposition type. 4. Add detailed notes. 5. Set follow-up date. 6. Save. Dispositions are tracked for audit and management reporting.

reprice_methodology.description: Scenario definition, pricing logic, negotiation guardrails

reprice_triggers: Gross margin below 20% for 2+ consecutive months
Bureau COGS increased significantly since last pricing review
Customer pull volume substantially exceeds original pricing assumptions
Legacy pricing (signed 2+ years ago) significantly below current rate card
Tier features have expanded but price has not been adjusted

margin_recovery.trigger: Margin below target, customer within normal parameters
margin_recovery.approach: Value-based reprice anchored to features delivered and market rates
margin_recovery.communication: Frame as market rate alignment, not a punishment. Lead with value delivered.
margin_recovery.template: Hi [Name], as part of our regular account review, we've identified that your current pricing is below market rate for the features and pull volume you're using. We'd like to align your subscription to reflect the full value of [specific features]. Let's schedule a call to discuss options that work for both of us.

upsell_bundling.trigger: Customer using features from a higher tier without paying for it, or would benefit from tier upgrade
upsell_bundling.approach: Position as tier upgrade with added features, not price increase
upsell_bundling.communication: Frame as unlocking more value. Show ROI of additional features.
upsell_bundling.template: Hi [Name], based on your usage patterns, you're already getting value from several [Next Tier] features. By upgrading your tier, you'd also unlock [specific features] which could help with [specific business outcome]. Let's review the numbers together.

product_swap.trigger: Customer using product mix that creates margin inefficiency
product_swap.approach: Restructure product configuration to optimize both cost and value
product_swap.communication: Frame as optimization, not change for change's sake.
product_swap.template: Hi [Name], we've been reviewing your product configuration and found some opportunities to optimize. By adjusting [specific change], we can improve [specific benefit] while better aligning your costs. I'd like to walk through the details.

utilization_adjustment.trigger: Customer consistently over-utilizing against CPQ allotment
utilization_adjustment.approach: Right-size the subscription to match actual usage
utilization_adjustment.communication: Frame as right-sizing to protect the customer from overage fees or service limitations.
utilization_adjustment.template: Hi [Name], your team's usage has been consistently above your current allotment, which is great news — it means you're getting real value. Let's adjust your plan to match your actual needs so you're covered and optimized.

bureau_shift.trigger: Customer's bureau mix has shifted toward higher-cost patterns
bureau_shift.approach: Bureau optimization conversation paired with potential reprice
bureau_shift.communication: Lead with savings opportunity, not cost increase.
bureau_shift.template: Hi [Name], we've been analyzing your credit pull patterns and found an opportunity to reduce your bureau costs by [estimated savings]. I'd like to walk through a bureau optimization strategy — it could save your dealership money while maintaining the same data quality.

negative_margin_emergency.trigger: Customer at negative margin — Informativ is losing money on the account
negative_margin_emergency.approach: Direct, honest conversation led by CS Manager. No soft framing.
negative_margin_emergency.communication: Transparent: the current pricing is unsustainable and must be adjusted.
negative_margin_emergency.escalation: CS Manager (Kiristen) leads. CRO (Michael) involved for Tier I accounts. Legal review if contract terms are relevant.

never_do: Never advise a customer on contract termination
Never approve a price that results in margin below 15% without CS Manager approval
Never propose a price increase >25% in a single conversation without CS Manager approval
Never discuss specific bureau cost rates with customers (internal confidential)
Never commit to pricing on the spot — always review with CS Manager first
always_do: Always pair price changes with value justification
Always provide data from CI Dashboard to support the conversation
Always give the customer options (e.g., tier upgrade vs. straight reprice)
Always document the conversation and outcome in CI Dashboard disposition
Always follow up in writing after verbal agreement

escalation_thresholds.csm_authority: Can propose reprices up to 15% increase. Must document in CI Dashboard.
escalation_thresholds.cs_manager_required: Reprices >15%, any negative margin account, any Tier I account, any customer threatening to cancel.
escalation_thresholds.cro_required: Tier I accounts with >25% proposed increase, negative margin accounts requiring contract renegotiation.

communication_templates.description: Core templates for CS team communications

health_alert.trigger: Customer health score drops to amber or red
health_alert.subject: Quick Check-In — [Dealership Name] Account Review
health_alert.body: Hi [Name], I was reviewing your account and noticed a few areas where we might be able to help optimize your results. I'd love to schedule a quick 15-minute call to review [specific dimension: utilization/margin/engagement] and make sure you're getting maximum value. When works best this week?
health_alert.follow_up: If no response in 3 business days, call directly. If no response after call + email, escalate to CS Manager.

upsell_pitch.trigger: Cross-sell score >70 or specific expansion signal identified
upsell_pitch.subject: New Capability That Could Help [Dealership Name]
upsell_pitch.body: Hi [Name], based on how your team is using [current features], I think [proposed feature/tier] could add significant value — specifically around [business outcome]. I've seen similar dealerships [proof point]. Would you be open to a 20-minute demo to see if it's a fit?
upsell_pitch.follow_up: Schedule demo within 2 weeks. Prepare ROI calculation beforehand.

margin_recovery_opening.subject: Account Optimization Opportunity — [Dealership Name]
margin_recovery_opening.body: Hi [Name], as part of our commitment to making sure your Informativ subscription delivers maximum value, I've been reviewing your account metrics. I've identified some opportunities to optimize both your costs and your experience. I'd like to schedule a call to walk through my findings and discuss options. Are you available [suggested times]?
margin_recovery_opening.follow_up: Prepare CI Dashboard data before call. Have reprice scenarios ready.

cancellation_save.trigger: Customer expresses intent to cancel or not renew
cancellation_save.role: CSM (credit-tier), CSA (flat-rate), escalate to CS Manager
cancellation_save.subject: Let's Talk — [Dealership Name]
cancellation_save.body: Hi [Name], I understand there are some concerns about your Informativ subscription. Before any decisions are made, I'd really value the chance to understand what's driving this and explore whether we can address it. Can we schedule a call this week? I want to make sure we've explored every option.
cancellation_save.follow_up: Escalate to CS Manager immediately. Prepare retention offer options before call.

reprice_notification.subject: Updated Subscription Details — [Dealership Name]
reprice_notification.body: Hi [Name], thank you for the productive conversation about your account. As discussed, here's a summary of the changes: [specific changes]. These take effect [date]. The changes reflect [value justification]. Please don't hesitate to reach out with any questions. We're committed to making sure you get maximum value from your investment.
reprice_notification.follow_up: Confirm implementation of price change with Revenue Operations.

utilization_warning.subject: Getting More From Your Informativ Investment
utilization_warning.body: Hi [Name], I noticed your team might not be taking full advantage of some features that could really help your operation — specifically [underutilized features]. Many dealerships find these save significant time and money once adopted. I'd love to schedule a quick training refresher. Would [date/time] work?
utilization_warning.follow_up: Schedule training session within 2 weeks. Document training outcome.

nps_detractor_response.subject: Your Feedback Matters — [Dealership Name]
nps_detractor_response.body: Hi [Name], thank you for your honest feedback on our recent survey. I take your experience seriously, and I'd like to personally understand what we can do better. Can I call you [today/tomorrow] to discuss? Your input directly shapes how we improve.
nps_detractor_response.follow_up: Call within 24 hours. Document root cause and remediation plan in CI Dashboard.

quarterly_business_review.trigger: Quarterly cadence (every 90 days for all accounts)
quarterly_business_review.subject: Q[X] Business Review — [Dealership Name]
quarterly_business_review.body: Hi [Name], it's time for our quarterly review. I've prepared a summary of your key metrics — health score, utilization, and ROI highlights. I'd also like to discuss any upcoming needs and how we can continue supporting your growth. Can we schedule 30 minutes in the next two weeks?
quarterly_business_review.follow_up: Prepare QBR deck with CI Dashboard data before meeting.

new_feature_announcement.trigger: Product release relevant to customer's tier/needs
new_feature_announcement.subject: New Feature Available — [Feature Name]
new_feature_announcement.body: Hi [Name], I wanted to let you know about a new capability we've just released: [feature description]. Based on your dealership's workflow, I think this could help with [specific benefit]. It's included in your current tier, so no additional cost. Want me to walk your team through it?
new_feature_announcement.follow_up: Schedule training if customer is interested.

bureau_optimization_outreach.trigger: Bureau Intelligence tab shows optimization opportunity
bureau_optimization_outreach.subject: Cost Savings Opportunity — Credit Pull Optimization
bureau_optimization_outreach.body: Hi [Name], I've been analyzing your credit pull patterns and found an opportunity to reduce your bureau costs without impacting data quality. Specifically, [1-2 sentence summary of opportunity]. I'd like to walk through the numbers — it could save your dealership [estimated savings]. Are you available for a quick call?
bureau_optimization_outreach.follow_up: Prepare bureau mix analysis from CI Dashboard before call.

negotiation_guardrails.description: Margin floors, tier migration rules, escalation criteria

content.pricing_philosophy: Informativ's pricing philosophy centers on value-based pricing anchored to business outcomes (Make More / Save More / Stay Safe), not cost-plus. The SaaS-first tier architecture ensures every customer starts on the compliance platform, creating a value floor that justifies baseline pricing. Price should always be defended with value evidence, not conceded under pressure.

margin_floors.absolute_floor: No account should remain below 10% gross margin for more than 60 days without active remediation plan and CS Manager approval.
margin_floors.target_floor: 27% gross margin for credit-tier customers. Accounts below 20% require active reprice plan.
margin_floors.flat_rate_floor: Flat-rate accounts should be reviewed annually to ensure pricing reflects current product costs. No specific margin floor but negative margin triggers same escalation as credit-tier.

upgrades.authority: CSA can recommend. CSM can propose. Sales team processes. CS Manager approves if pricing deviated from rate card.
triggers: Customer using features from higher tier
Bureau optimization opportunity that benefits from higher tier
upgrades.process: 1. Identify upgrade signal → 2. Prepare ROI justification → 3. Present to customer → 4. If agreed, coordinate with Sales/RevOps for contract amendment → 5. Document in CI Dashboard

downgrades.authority: Always escalate to CS Manager (Kiristen). Never process a downgrade without CS Manager review.
triggers: Retention negotiation where downgrade prevents churn
downgrades.process: 1. Customer requests downgrade → 2. Escalate to CS Manager → 3. CS Manager evaluates retention risk vs. revenue impact → 4. If approved, coordinate with RevOps → 5. Document reason and retention outcome

cs_manager_kiristen: Any Tier I account (≥$100K ARR) with pricing or retention issues
cro_michael: Tier I accounts with >25% proposed increase
Negative margin accounts requiring contract renegotiation
Competitive intelligence updates that affect CS positioning
director_client_ops: DNM cases that exhaust RSM escalation path
High-risk DNM customers at ELT level
Cross-functional process issues affecting CS delivery

competitive_positioning.when_customer_mentions_competitor: Never disparage the competitor. Instead: acknowledge they're solid tools, then position Informativ's unique value — unified platform (credit + compliance + fraud + payments in one), forced-march compliance, and proven ROI proof points.
key_differentiators_for_cs: End-to-end platform vs. point solution (one login, one invoice, one partner)
Revenue enablement + compliance in one platform (SmartPencil + compliance enforcement)
Proven fraud prevention (5,500+ fake IDs flagged, $100M+ protected)
FTC Safeguards enforcement that competitors don't offer at the same level
competitive_positioning.rep_vs_cs_language: CS team uses relationship language, not sales language. Instead of 'closing' use 'aligning.' Instead of 'objection handling' use 'addressing concerns.' Instead of 'pitch' use 'recommendation.' The goal is trusted advisor, not salesperson.

bureau_optimization.description: Bureau mix rebalancing, SmartPull configuration, cost vs. coverage

3_to_2_bureau_shift.description: Many dealers habitually pull all 3 bureaus when 1-2 would suffice. Reducing from 3-bureau to 2-bureau pulls can significantly reduce COGS.
3_to_2_bureau_shift.approach: Review customer's lender requirements. Most lenders only need 1-2 bureaus. Identify which bureau is redundant.
3_to_2_bureau_shift.savings_estimate: Reducing from 3-bureau to 2-bureau on 50% of pulls can improve margin by 3-5 percentage points.
3_to_2_bureau_shift.customer_pitch: We've analyzed your pull patterns and found that [X]% of your deals could use a 2-bureau pull without impacting lender funding. That could save your dealership approximately $[amount]/month in credit costs.

soft_pull_adoption.description: Converting qualifying scenarios from hard pulls to soft pulls. Soft pulls cost significantly less and don't impact customer credit scores.
soft_pull_adoption.approach: Identify deal stages where soft pull provides sufficient data (pre-qualification, payment scenarios, initial desk). Reserve hard pulls for lender submission.
soft_pull_adoption.savings_estimate: Converting 30-50% of pulls to soft can improve margin by 5-10 percentage points.
soft_pull_adoption.customer_pitch: With our soft pull strategy, your team can pre-qualify customers and generate real payment scenarios without hitting their credit. It's faster for the desk, better for the customer, and reduces your credit costs.

smartpull_routing.description: SmartPull automatically routes credit requests to the lowest-cost bureau that meets the lender's requirements.
smartpull_routing.approach: Configure SmartPull preferences based on dealer's primary lenders and bureau requirements.
smartpull_routing.benefit: Automated cost optimization without any change to dealer workflow.

bureau_mix_rebalancing.description: Shifting pull distribution across bureaus based on cost and lender preference.
bureau_mix_rebalancing.approach: Analyze which bureau is pulled most frequently. Compare with lender requirements. Recommend shifting volume to lower-cost bureau where acceptable.
bureau_mix_rebalancing.data_source: CI Dashboard Bureau Intelligence tab provides per-bureau pull counts, costs, and trends.

cost_vs_coverage_framework.principle: The goal is not to minimize pulls — it's to eliminate unnecessary pulls while maintaining data quality and lender compliance.

decision_matrix.pre_qualification: Soft pull only. No hard pull needed at this stage.
decision_matrix.payment_scenario: Soft pull via SmartPencil. Real score used on backend.
decision_matrix.desk_decision: Soft pull data usually sufficient. Hard pull only if lender requires it at this stage.
decision_matrix.lender_submission: Hard pull required. Pull only the bureau(s) the specific lender requires.
decision_matrix.deal_finalization: Hard pull already completed at lender submission. No additional pulls needed.

bureau_conversations.description: CSA language for bureau change recommendations, usage justifications

conversation_framework.opening: When recommending bureau changes, always lead with the dealer's benefit — cost savings or efficiency — not Informativ's margin. The dealer should never feel like you're restricting their access.
key_principles: Use data from CI Dashboard to support recommendations
Acknowledge the dealer's current workflow and habits
Explain the 'why' — not just the 'what' to change
Offer to pilot the change with a subset of deals first

3_bureau_to_2_bureau.opening: I've been looking at your credit pull patterns, and I noticed your team is pulling all three bureaus on about [X]% of deals. For most of your primary lenders, two bureaus give them everything they need to make a funding decision.
3_bureau_to_2_bureau.value_prop: By adjusting to a 2-bureau default for standard deals, you'd save approximately $[amount] per month — and your lenders get the same data they need.
3_bureau_to_2_bureau.objection_if_resistance: I totally understand the habit of pulling all three — it feels like more data = better. But here's the thing: most lenders only underwrite off one or two bureaus anyway. The third pull is an unnecessary cost that doesn't improve your funding odds.
3_bureau_to_2_bureau.close: How about we try this on your standard deals for 30 days and compare the results? If you see any impact on funding rates, we'll switch right back.

soft_pull_adoption.opening: One of the biggest wins I've seen dealerships get is using soft pulls for pre-qualification. Your team can qualify buyers and generate payment scenarios without ever hitting their credit — which customers love.
soft_pull_adoption.value_prop: The soft pull costs a fraction of a hard pull, gives you real credit data for SmartPencil, and customers don't see a credit hit. It's a better experience all around.
soft_pull_adoption.objection_if_resistance: Some dealers worry the soft pull isn't accurate enough. But SmartPencil uses a real credit score on the backend — the payment scenarios are credit-qualified, not estimates.
soft_pull_adoption.close: Let's activate soft pulls for your pre-qualification workflow and see how your team likes it. Most dealers find it speeds up their desk and customers appreciate the lighter touch.

excessive_pulling.opening: I noticed your team's pull volume has been trending up over the past few months. That's a sign of good activity, but I want to make sure every pull is productive.
excessive_pulling.value_prop: We can set up Credit Guardrails that alert your managers when pull patterns look unusual — like multiple hard pulls on the same customer or pulls that don't convert to deals. It's a cost control that also gives you visibility into team habits.
excessive_pulling.close: Want me to walk through the Guardrail setup? It takes about 10 minutes and gives your managers real-time visibility.

csa_vs_csm_approach.csa: CSAs focus on feature adoption and training. Bureau conversations are framed as helping the dealer get more value from the platform. Keep it educational, not financial.
csa_vs_csm_approach.csm: CSMs connect bureau optimization to margin outcomes. Bureau conversations are paired with pricing discussions. Keep it data-driven and ROI-focused.

cpq_guardrail_rules.description: Usage thresholds, alert triggers, CSA intervention points

content.cpq_overview: CPQ (Configure, Price, Quote) allotments define what each customer is entitled to under their subscription. The CI Dashboard CPQ Alerts tab tracks actual usage against these allotments and generates alerts when customers approach or exceed limits.

credit_pull_guardrails.description: Limits on total credit pulls (hard + soft) per billing period

alert_levels.amber: Usage 75-90% of allotment — proactive outreach recommended
alert_levels.red: Usage 90%+ of allotment — immediate intervention required

credit_pull_guardrails.intervention: Amber: reach out to discuss usage patterns and optimization. Red: discuss right-sizing the plan or adjusting pull behavior.

bureau_count_guardrails.description: Alerts when customer's bureau mix exceeds expected patterns
bureau_count_guardrails.trigger: Consistently pulling 3 bureaus when plan is priced for 1-2 bureau average

product_usage_guardrails.description: Tracks utilization of included product features (TurboPass, SmartPencil, etc.)

alert_levels.under_utilization: Using <40% of included features — training/adoption needed
alert_levels.over_utilization: Exceeding included allotment — plan upgrade needed

product_usage_guardrails.intervention: Under: schedule feature training. Over: discuss plan upgrade.

csa_intervention_points.proactive_alerts: CSA reviews CPQ Alerts tab weekly. Any amber or red alerts should be addressed within 5 business days.
csa_intervention_points.quarterly_allotment_review: Every quarter, review each customer's allotment vs. actual usage. Identify customers consistently over or under their allotment.
csa_intervention_points.overage_prevention: Goal is to prevent overages before they happen, not bill for them after. Proactive right-sizing creates trust and prevents billing disputes.
csa_intervention_points.escalation: If customer refuses to right-size despite consistent overages, escalate to CS Manager for pricing decision.

content.quarter_fallback_note: On the first day of a new quarter, the CPQ tab has no data for the current quarter yet. The scoring engine automatically falls back to the most recent complete quarter and shows an amber banner. No manual intervention needed — data appears as soon as new quarter pulls accumulate.

install_workflows.description: Installation scenarios, product bundling, feature sequencing

content.installation_overview: New customer installations follow a structured workflow from Closed Won through first-value milestone. The target is first-value within 14 days. The installation sequence varies by tier — higher tiers have more features to configure but the core compliance setup is always first.

step_1_kick_off.description: Implementation Kick-Off call with customer, CSA, and RSM
activities: Introductions and relationship handoff from Sales to CS
Confirm product tier and feature scope
Set expectations for timeline and milestones
step_1_kick_off.timeline: Within 3 business days of Closed Won

step_2_compliance_setup.description: Core compliance platform activation — this is always first regardless of tier
activities: Set up Adverse Action Letter Fulfillment

step_3_credit_configuration.description: Credit pull setup and bureau configuration
activities: Connect to credit bureaus (Equifax, Experian, TransUnion as contracted)
Configure hard pull and soft pull settings
Configure credit guardrails (if tier includes them)
Conduct training sessions for all relevant dealer staff
Set up sandbox/practice environment if available
Confirm all features active and working

dnm_risk_management.ce3_process: If customer is unresponsive during implementation: 3 phone calls per week + supplementary emails for initial 2 weeks.
dnm_risk_management.escalation_timeline: 2 weeks no contact → RSM notified → 4 weeks → On Hold (DNM Risk) → 6 months max → DNM classification → bookings removed, commissions clawed back.
dnm_risk_management.prevention: CSA involvement from Day 1 reduces DNM risk. The key is establishing relationship and demonstrating value quickly.

product_bundling_considerations.compliance_first: Always install compliance features before credit. This establishes the compliance value story and prevents 'just credit pulls' perception.
product_bundling_considerations.payment_desking: SmartPencil configuration requires lender program matching — allow extra time for payment engine calibration.
product_bundling_considerations.multi_rooftop: Enterprise installations require per-rooftop configuration but benefit from group-level templates and policies.

utilization_thresholds.description: QTD/monthly actual vs. CPQ allotment, underutilization alerts

optimal_range.interpretation: Customer is getting good value from subscription. Healthy engagement.
optimal_range.action: Monitor quarterly. Identify expansion opportunities if trending toward upper end.

under_utilization_mild.interpretation: Customer may not be fully aware of available features or may need training refresher.
under_utilization_mild.action: Schedule proactive check-in. Identify unused features. Offer training.

under_utilization_severe.interpretation: Significant churn risk. Customer is not getting value from the investment.
under_utilization_severe.action: Immediate outreach. Schedule training session. Identify adoption blockers. Escalate if Tier I.

over_utilization_mild.interpretation: Customer approaching limits. Good engagement but may need plan upgrade.
over_utilization_mild.action: Proactive notification. Discuss right-sizing options before overage occurs.

over_utilization_severe.interpretation: Customer exceeding allotment. Margin risk for Informativ. Customer may face service limitations.
over_utilization_severe.action: Immediate conversation about plan upgrade. Frame as right-sizing to protect their service continuity.

under_utilization_investigation.step_1: Review CI Dashboard for which specific features are underused
under_utilization_investigation.step_2: Check engagement dimension — are they logging in? Or just not using features?
under_utilization_investigation.step_3: Identify potential causes: lack of training, staff turnover, process change, feature not relevant
under_utilization_investigation.step_4: Schedule call with dealer contact to understand barriers
under_utilization_investigation.step_5: Build adoption plan targeting specific underused features

feature_adoption_playbook.compliance_features: If compliance features underused: remind dealer of FTC Safeguards requirements and real-world enforcement examples. Frame compliance tools as risk mitigation.
feature_adoption_playbook.smartpencil: If SmartPencil underused: offer demo refresher. Show how it speeds up desk workflow. Address common objection: 'we already have a desking tool.'
feature_adoption_playbook.soft_pull: If soft pulls underused: explain customer experience benefit (no credit hit) and cost benefit (lower per-pull cost). Walk through soft pull workflow.
feature_adoption_playbook.turbopass: If TurboPass underused: highlight recontract reduction proof point (45% fewer recontracts). Show specific report types and how they prevent deal fallout.
feature_adoption_playbook.credit_guardrails: If Credit Guardrails underused: show how they give managers visibility into credit spend. Frame as management tool, not restriction.

smartpull_optimization.description: For customers using SmartPull, optimize configuration to reduce costs while maintaining data quality.
steps: Adjust routing to prefer lower-cost bureaus where acceptable
Monitor for 30 days to confirm no impact on funding rates

bureau_mix_rebalancing_playbook.description: For customers with inefficient bureau mix patterns.
steps: Pull bureau mix data from CI Dashboard Bureau Intelligence tab

cross_sell_identification.description: Expansion signals, tier upgrade opportunities, feature adoption paths

tier_upgrade_signals: Customer consistently using 80%+ of current tier's features
Customer asking about features in next tier
Customer's pull volume exceeding tier allotment
Customer's rooftop count increasing (needs multi-rooftop features)
Customer expressing interest in SmartPencil or soft pulls (if not in current tier)
product_expansion_signals: Customer mentions income verification challenges → TurboPass opportunity
Customer mentions payment accuracy issues → SmartPencil opportunity
Customer mentions fraud concerns → fraud prevention suite upgrade
Customer mentions compliance audit coming → compliance enforcement upgrade
Customer mentions credit cost concerns → soft pull + Credit Guardrails
behavioral_signals: Increasing login frequency and feature exploration
Requesting training on features not in their current tier
Asking about integration capabilities they don't currently have
Referring other dealerships to Informativ (promoter behavior)

qualification_framework.step_1_identify: Use CI Dashboard Cross-Sell tab to identify accounts with high cross-sell scores (>70).
qualification_framework.step_2_validate: Review the specific signals. Is the customer actively demonstrating need, or is it a scoring artifact?
qualification_framework.step_3_prepare: Build ROI case for the expansion. Use proof points from similar dealerships.
qualification_framework.step_4_present: Schedule conversation with customer. Lead with their business outcome, not the product.
qualification_framework.step_5_handoff: If cross-sell involves significant pricing change: CSA hands off to CSM (credit-tier) or coordinates with Sales team.

structured_sales_handoff.when_to_hand_off: Any expansion involving new contract terms, significant pricing changes, or products outside CS authority to quote.
handoff_process: Document the opportunity in CI Dashboard disposition (type: Expansion)
Include: customer name, current tier, proposed expansion, signals observed, customer contact preferences
Notify assigned RSM or Sales team with written summary
CSA/CSM stays involved as trusted advisor during sales conversation
After close, CSA manages implementation of new features
structured_sales_handoff.warm_handoff_script: Hi [RSM Name], I've been working with [Dealer Name] and they've been showing strong interest in [feature/tier]. They're currently on [current tier] and their utilization suggests they'd benefit from [proposed tier/feature]. I've already laid the groundwork — they understand the value and asked about next steps. Here are the key contacts and their priorities: [details].

feature_adoption_paths.protect_to_enrich: Customer is compliance-mature and wants to speed up deal desking → SmartPencil adds payment accuracy and desk speed.
feature_adoption_paths.enrich_to_elevate: Customer wants score visibility and credit cost control → Soft pulls + Credit Guardrails add prequalification and cost management.
feature_adoption_paths.elevate_to_control: Customer expanding to multi-rooftop or needs enterprise reporting → Full platform control and visibility.
feature_adoption_paths.any_tier_to_turbopass: Customer experiencing recontracts or funding delays → TurboPass income/identity verification.
feature_adoption_paths.any_tier_to_smartpencil_training: Customer has SmartPencil but low adoption → Training refresher focused on desk workflow integration.

_module_m1_reprice_scenarios.title: Reprice Scenarios — Deep Dive
_module_m1_reprice_scenarios.description: Comprehensive reprice playbook for CSMs. 8 common scenarios with communication sequences, negotiation guardrails, and success metrics.

scenario_1_margin_recovery_standard.name: Standard Margin Recovery
scenario_1_margin_recovery_standard.trigger: Customer margin 15-22% for 2+ months, no other complicating factors

approach.step_1_prepare: Pull T12 financials from CI Dashboard detail panel. Calculate exact margin gap. Identify revenue needed: Current COGS / (1 - 0.27) = Required Revenue.
approach.step_2_frame: Position as market rate alignment. Lead with value delivered since last pricing review. Reference new features added, bureau cost changes, market conditions.

step_3_communicate.channel: Phone call first, followed by email summary
step_3_communicate.opening: Hi [Name], as part of our regular account review, I've been looking at your metrics and wanted to discuss ensuring your subscription reflects the full value you're receiving.
step_3_communicate.value_anchor: Since your last pricing review, we've added [specific features/improvements]. Your team is actively using [list features], which is generating real value in [compliance/speed/fraud prevention].
step_3_communicate.price_introduction: To align your subscription with current market rates and the expanded value you're receiving, we'd like to adjust your monthly rate from $[current] to $[proposed]. This reflects a [X]% adjustment.
step_3_communicate.close: I'd love to walk through the numbers with you and discuss what option works best. Can we schedule 20 minutes this week?

approach.step_4_negotiate: If pushback: offer options — straight price increase OR tier upgrade that adds features to justify the higher price. Always have 2-3 options prepared.
approach.step_5_document: Log disposition in CI Dashboard: Margin Action → Reprice Initiated. Record proposed amount, customer response, and follow-up date.
approach.step_6_follow_up: Written confirmation within 24 hours of verbal agreement. Coordinate with RevOps for billing change.

scenario_1_margin_recovery_standard.escalation: If customer refuses and margin stays below 20%, escalate to CS Manager

scenario_2_upsell_bundling.name: Upsell Bundle Reprice
scenario_2_upsell_bundling.trigger: Customer using features from higher tier, or would clearly benefit from tier upgrade
scenario_2_upsell_bundling.csm_authority: Yes — framed as upgrade, not increase

approach.step_1_prepare: Identify which higher-tier features the customer is either using informally or would benefit from. Build ROI case for the upgrade.
approach.step_2_frame: Position as unlocking more value, not increasing cost. The price increase is justified by new features.

step_3_communicate.opening: Hi [Name], I've been reviewing your account and noticed your team is already getting value from several capabilities that are part of our [Next Tier] package.
step_3_communicate.value_anchor: By moving to [Next Tier], you'd unlock [Feature 1], [Feature 2], and [Feature 3] — which based on your usage patterns could [specific business outcome].
step_3_communicate.price_introduction: The upgrade would adjust your monthly investment from $[current] to $[proposed], but the added capabilities typically deliver [ROI metric] in return.
step_3_communicate.close: Want me to walk through a side-by-side comparison of what you have now versus what [Next Tier] includes?

approach.step_4_negotiate: If price is the concern, emphasize ROI per feature. Show what they're already doing manually that the new tier automates.

success_metrics: Customer using new features within 30 days

scenario_3_product_swap.name: Product Configuration Swap
scenario_3_product_swap.trigger: Customer's product mix creating margin inefficiency — wrong products for their use case

approach.step_1_prepare: Analyze which products contribute to margin drag. Identify alternative configuration that better matches usage.
approach.step_2_frame: Position as optimization — better fit for their needs, not a downgrade or change for change's sake.

step_3_communicate.opening: Hi [Name], I've been looking at your product configuration and found an opportunity to optimize how your subscription is set up.
step_3_communicate.value_anchor: Right now you have [current config]. Based on how your team actually uses the platform, a [proposed config] would better match your workflow and could [specific benefit].
step_3_communicate.close: I'd like to walk through the details — it's a simple adjustment that could improve your experience and save you money.

scenario_4_utilization_adjustment.name: Right-Size for Over-Utilization
scenario_4_utilization_adjustment.trigger: Customer consistently exceeding CPQ allotment (100%+ utilization for 2+ months)

approach.step_1_prepare: Pull CPQ data from CI Dashboard. Document actual vs. allotted usage. Calculate overage cost impact.
approach.step_2_frame: Positive framing — their team is getting great value, now let's make sure the plan matches reality.

step_3_communicate.opening: Hi [Name], great news — your team's usage has been consistently above your current plan, which tells me they're getting real value from the platform.
step_3_communicate.value_anchor: To make sure you're covered and don't run into any service limitations, I'd recommend adjusting your plan to match your actual usage.
step_3_communicate.price_introduction: Based on your current volume, the right-sized plan would be $[proposed]/month. This gives you headroom for your typical usage plus a buffer.
step_3_communicate.close: This protects you from overages and ensures your team always has what they need. Can I send over the details?

scenario_5_bureau_shift_reprice.name: Bureau-Driven Reprice
scenario_5_bureau_shift_reprice.trigger: Customer's bureau mix shifted to higher-cost patterns since original pricing

approach.step_1_prepare: Pull bureau mix data from CI Dashboard Bureau Intelligence tab. Calculate cost difference between current mix and original pricing assumptions.
approach.step_2_frame: Lead with savings opportunity (bureau optimization), then address pricing alignment.

step_3_dual_approach.part_a_optimize: First recommend bureau optimization — if they shift from 3-bureau to 2-bureau or increase soft pull adoption, that alone may close the margin gap.
step_3_dual_approach.part_b_reprice_if_needed: If optimization doesn't fully close the gap, then discuss pricing adjustment to reflect the actual bureau cost structure.

step_3_communicate.opening: Hi [Name], I've been analyzing your credit pull patterns and found both a cost savings opportunity and something we need to discuss about your pricing.
step_3_communicate.value_anchor: The good news: by optimizing your bureau mix, we can save your dealership approximately $[savings]/month. The adjustment: your current pull patterns have shifted since your original pricing, so we need to align your subscription accordingly.
step_3_communicate.close: Let me walk through both sides — the savings and the adjustment. Net-net, the impact is much smaller than it might seem.

scenario_6_legacy_pricing_alignment.name: Legacy Pricing Catch-Up
scenario_6_legacy_pricing_alignment.trigger: Customer signed 2+ years ago at rates significantly below current rate card
scenario_6_legacy_pricing_alignment.csm_authority: Yes if <15% increase; CS Manager for larger

approach.step_1_prepare: Compare customer's current rate to current rate card. Document all features and improvements added since their original signing.
approach.step_2_frame: Value growth framing — they've been receiving increasing value at the same price. This is an alignment, not a penalty.

step_3_communicate.opening: Hi [Name], you've been with us for [X years] and we really value that partnership. As part of our annual review process, I want to discuss an update to your subscription.
step_3_communicate.value_anchor: Since you signed, we've added [list major features: SmartPencil, enhanced fraud detection, Credit Guardrails, etc.]. Your team is actively using [specific features], which weren't in your original package.
step_3_communicate.price_introduction: To reflect the expanded platform you're using today, we'd like to adjust your rate from $[current] to $[proposed]. This still represents strong value compared to our current rate card.
step_3_communicate.close: I appreciate your loyalty and want to make sure this works. Can we discuss options?

success_metrics: Pricing aligned to within 10% of rate card

scenario_7_negative_margin_emergency.name: Negative Margin Emergency
scenario_7_negative_margin_emergency.trigger: Customer at negative margin — Informativ is losing money on the account
scenario_7_negative_margin_emergency.csm_authority: NO — CS Manager (Kiristen) leads. CRO (Michael) involved for Tier I.

approach.step_1_escalate_immediately: Do NOT attempt to handle independently. Notify CS Manager with: customer name, current margin, duration of negative margin, estimated monthly loss, and any known context.
approach.step_2_cs_manager_leads: CS Manager prepares the conversation. Assembles options: aggressive reprice, tier restructure, bureau optimization, or managed exit.
approach.step_3_direct_conversation: Transparent communication — the current pricing is unsustainable. This is a partnership conversation, not a punitive one.
approach.step_4_nma_freeze_protocol: If customer cannot or will not adjust: NMA (Negative Margin Analysis) freeze protocol. Account flagged, billing reviewed, contract terms examined.
approach.step_5_cro_involvement: For Tier I accounts or if the customer threatens cancellation, CRO (Michael) joins the conversation.

guardrails: NEVER handle negative margin alone — always escalate
NEVER threaten service reduction — let CS Manager decide approach
NEVER disclose specific bureau cost rates
success_metrics: Margin restored to positive within 90 days

scenario_8_renewal_reprice.name: Renewal-Triggered Reprice
scenario_8_renewal_reprice.trigger: Contract renewal approaching (90 days out) and current pricing is below target margin

approach.step_1_prepare_early: Begin renewal preparation 90 days before expiry. Pull T12 data, calculate margin, identify any pricing misalignment.
approach.step_2_build_renewal_package: Assemble: usage data, ROI proof points, new features delivered during contract term, industry benchmarks.
approach.step_3_frame_as_renewal_conversation: This is a normal renewal discussion, not a confrontation. Price adjustments at renewal are expected by most customers.

step_3_communicate.opening: Hi [Name], your renewal is coming up in [X weeks] and I wanted to get ahead of it. I've prepared a summary of your account performance and some options for your next term.
step_3_communicate.value_anchor: Over the past [term], your team has [key usage metrics]. You've [specific value outcomes]. The platform has grown significantly with [new features added].
step_3_communicate.price_introduction: For your renewal, we're proposing [rate] which reflects your current usage and the expanded platform. I have a couple of options to discuss.
step_3_communicate.close: Can we schedule time next week to walk through the renewal package together?

_module_m2_utilization_troubleshooting.title: Utilization Issues & Troubleshooting — Deep Dive
_module_m2_utilization_troubleshooting.description: Decision trees by product, remediation workflows, and handoff procedures for utilization issues.

symptoms: Pull volume below 40% of allotment
Customer only using hard pulls (no soft pull adoption)
Pulls concentrated on single bureau when multi-bureau included
diagnosis_questions: Are all staff trained on the credit pull workflow?
Has there been staff turnover since implementation?
Are they aware soft pulls are available?
Are they still using a secondary credit provider alongside Informativ?
Has their deal volume decreased?

remediation.training_gap: Schedule 30-min refresher training focused on credit workflow. Include soft pull demonstration.
remediation.staff_turnover: Schedule new-hire onboarding session. Provide quick-reference guide. Identify new power users.
remediation.dual_provider: Address directly — understand why they're splitting volume. Usually cost perception or habit. Show consolidated value.
remediation.volume_decline: This is a business health signal, not a product issue. Flag for CSM review — potential churn risk.

symptoms: Pull volume exceeding allotment by 10%+
Excessive 3-bureau pulling
Multiple hard pulls per customer (same customer, same day)
Pull-to-deal ratio above 3:1
diagnosis_questions: Are desk managers aware of the credit guardrail settings?
Are they pulling multiple bureaus out of habit or lender requirement?
Are there duplicate pulls from staff sharing logins?
Has their deal volume increased significantly?

remediation.habit_pulling: Bureau optimization conversation — show 3-to-2 shift savings. Reference bureau_conversations talk tracks.
remediation.guardrail_setup: Configure Credit Guardrails to alert on excessive pulling. Frame as management visibility tool.
remediation.volume_growth: Positive signal — right-size the plan to accommodate growth. See reprice scenario_4_utilization_adjustment.
remediation.login_sharing: Address as compliance concern — shared logins create audit trail gaps. Set up individual user accounts.

symptoms: SmartPull configured but not routing optimally
Manual bureau selection overriding SmartPull recommendations
Soft pull not being used for pre-qualification
diagnosis_questions: Is SmartPull configured for their primary lenders?
Are desk managers overriding SmartPull selections?
Do they understand how SmartPull chooses the bureau?

remediation.configuration: Review SmartPull routing rules. Ensure lender preferences are current. Adjust routing to optimize for cost.
remediation.override_habit: Education — show desk managers the cost difference between manual selection and SmartPull optimization. Frame as 'let the system do the work.'
remediation.soft_pull_adoption: Demonstrate soft pull workflow in SmartPencil context. Show how pre-qualification leads to better desk outcomes.

optimization_checklist: Verify SmartPull lender configurations are current
Review override frequency — if >20% overrides, investigate why
Check soft pull vs. hard pull ratio — target 30%+ soft pull
Compare bureau costs before and after SmartPull activation
Monitor for 30 days after any configuration change

compliance_features.product: Compliance Suite (Red Flags, OFAC, Deal Jacket, Adverse Action)

symptoms: Digital deal jackets not being created for all deals
Red Flags checks being skipped or overridden
Adverse Action letters not being generated
Compliance dashboard not being monitored
diagnosis_questions: Are all sales staff using Informativ for every deal, or only some?
Is there a secondary paper-based process running in parallel?
Are managers monitoring the compliance dashboard?
Has there been a compliance audit or FTC concern recently?

remediation.partial_adoption: Frame as risk: any deal NOT in Informativ is an unprotected liability. Use $42,380 paper credit app liability stat.
remediation.parallel_process: Work with dealer to sunset paper process. Offer migration support. Show time savings of digital workflow.
remediation.manager_awareness: Set up compliance dashboard alerts. Schedule manager training on monitoring features.
remediation.audit_preparation: If audit is upcoming, position compliance features as audit defense. Offer compliance readiness review.

compliance_features.urgency_framing: Compliance under-utilization is a LIABILITY issue, not just a usage issue. Every deal not processed through Informativ's compliance suite is an unprotected exposure. Frame accordingly.

symptoms: SmartPencil included in tier but <20% of deals using it
Desk managers still using separate desking tool exclusively
Payment calculations not being shared with customers
Only loan scenarios used (no lease)
diagnosis_questions: Have all desk managers been trained on SmartPencil?
Is there resistance to changing the desking workflow?
Are they aware SmartPencil works alongside (not replaces) existing desking?
Are they using soft pull data to power SmartPencil scenarios?

remediation.training_needed: Schedule SmartPencil demo refresher. Focus on the 'cheatsheet' positioning — it complements their existing tools.
remediation.workflow_resistance: Address the 'we already have a desking tool' objection: SmartPencil finds the best payment intelligently, then they enter it into their existing system.
remediation.score_visibility_concern: If on Enrich (no score visibility): explain backend scoring accuracy. If they need score visibility, this is a tier upgrade signal (Elevate/Control).
remediation.lease_scenarios: Many desk managers default to loan only. Show lease scenario capability — residuals, money factors, multiple term permutations.

adoption_sequence: Week 1: Training session for all desk managers (30 min)
Week 2: Assign 'SmartPencil champion' on sales floor
Week 3: Check-in — review usage data, address blockers
Week 4: Expand to full team if champion adoption successful
Month 2: Review adoption metrics, celebrate wins, address remaining holdouts

symptoms: TurboPass included but <10% of deals using it
Only using one report type (e.g., only Banking, not Payroll)
Income verification still being done manually/paper
diagnosis_questions: Does the F&I team know TurboPass is available?
Are they still collecting paper paystubs manually?
How many recontracts per month are due to income verification issues?
Do they know about the different report types (Bravo, Alpha, Paystub, Insurance)?

remediation.awareness_gap: F&I team may not know they have TurboPass. Schedule feature introduction with F&I Director.
remediation.manual_habit: Show time savings: TurboPass pulls verified income in seconds vs. chasing paystubs for days. Use 45% recontract reduction proof point.
remediation.report_type_education: Walk through all report types and when to use each. Banking (Bravo) for income proof, Paystub for employment verification, Insurance for proof of insurance.
remediation.recontract_cost: Calculate their monthly recontract cost. Show how TurboPass adoption directly reduces this cost.

handoff_to_product_team.when: If a utilization issue is caused by a product bug, missing feature, or integration problem — not a training or adoption issue
