process: Document the specific issue with screenshots/steps to reproduce
Log a support ticket with Product/Engineering
Follow up until resolved, then re-engage on adoption

_module_m3_disposition_playbooks.title: Disposition Playbooks — Deep Dive
_module_m3_disposition_playbooks.description: 15 disposition types with role-specific language, urgency classification, and next actions. Each playbook provides CSM and CSA variants.

D01_quarterly_business_review.name: Quarterly Business Review (QBR)
D01_quarterly_business_review.csm_language: I've prepared your quarterly portfolio review with margin trends, bureau efficiency data, and pricing alignment analysis.
D01_quarterly_business_review.csa_language: I've prepared your quarterly review with utilization metrics, feature adoption progress, and some recommendations for getting even more value.
actions: Prepare QBR deck with CI Dashboard data
Document outcomes and set next QBR date
D01_quarterly_business_review.follow_up: 30 days for any action items, 90 days for next QBR

D02_health_score_decline.name: Health Score Decline Alert
D02_health_score_decline.trigger: Health score drops 10+ points in a single month OR enters red zone
D02_health_score_decline.csm_language: I noticed some changes in your account metrics that I want to address proactively — specifically around [margin/revenue/bureau efficiency].
D02_health_score_decline.csa_language: I noticed some changes in your usage patterns that I want to address — specifically around [utilization/engagement/feature adoption].
actions: Escalate to CS Manager if Tier I or red zone

D03_reprice_initiated.name: Reprice Conversation Initiated
D03_reprice_initiated.csm_language: As part of our account optimization review, I'd like to discuss aligning your subscription with the current value and market rates.
D03_reprice_initiated.csa_language: [CSA does not lead reprice conversations — hand off to CSM with documented signal]
actions: Select reprice scenario from M1 playbook
D03_reprice_initiated.follow_up: 48 hours after conversation for written confirmation

D04_bureau_optimization_recommended.name: Bureau Optimization Recommended
D04_bureau_optimization_recommended.trigger: Bureau Intelligence tab shows optimization opportunity (3-bureau habit, low soft pull adoption)
D04_bureau_optimization_recommended.csm_language: I've analyzed your credit pull patterns and found an opportunity to reduce your bureau costs while maintaining the same data quality for your lenders.
D04_bureau_optimization_recommended.csa_language: I've been looking at how your team uses credit pulls and found a way to streamline the process — it could save time and reduce costs.
actions: Prepare bureau mix analysis from CI Dashboard
Select appropriate talk track from bureau_conversations

D05_nma_freeze_applied.name: NMA Freeze Applied
D05_nma_freeze_applied.trigger: Negative margin account flagged for freeze protocol
D05_nma_freeze_applied.csm_language: [CS Manager leads this conversation — CSM supports with data preparation]
D05_nma_freeze_applied.csa_language: [Escalate to CS Manager immediately — do not handle independently]

D06_churn_risk_identified.name: Churn Risk Identified
D06_churn_risk_identified.trigger: Multiple churn signals present (declining health, low engagement, competitor mention, contact changes)
D06_churn_risk_identified.csm_language: I want to check in personally — your account is important to us and I want to make sure we're delivering the value you expect.
D06_churn_risk_identified.csa_language: I noticed we haven't connected recently and I want to make sure everything is working well for your team.
actions: Identify root cause of churn signals
Build retention plan with specific remediation steps
Escalate to CS Manager if Tier I

D07_retention_plan_active.name: Retention Plan Active
D07_retention_plan_active.trigger: Customer flagged for churn risk with active remediation plan
D07_retention_plan_active.csm_language: Following up on our remediation plan — I want to review progress on [specific items] and make sure we're on track.
D07_retention_plan_active.csa_language: Checking in on the action items we discussed — how is [specific item] working for your team?

D08_competitor_threat.name: Competitor Threat Documented
D08_competitor_threat.trigger: Customer mentions competitor by name or requests comparison
D08_competitor_threat.csm_language: I understand you're evaluating options. I'd love the chance to address any concerns directly and show you the full picture of what we deliver.
D08_competitor_threat.csa_language: I hear you — let me make sure you have a complete view of everything Informativ provides so you can make the best decision.
actions: Reference proof points (5,500+ fake IDs flagged, 45% fewer recontracts)

D09_tier_upgrade_proposed.name: Tier Upgrade Proposed
D09_tier_upgrade_proposed.trigger: Customer showing upgrade signals (high utilization, feature requests, growing rooftops)
D09_tier_upgrade_proposed.csm_language: Based on your usage and growth, I think there's an opportunity to unlock additional value with our [Next Tier] package.
D09_tier_upgrade_proposed.csa_language: Your team has been getting great results and I think you'd benefit from some additional capabilities we offer.
actions: Coordinate with Sales/RevOps for contract amendment

D10_cross_sell_opportunity.name: Cross-Sell Opportunity Identified
D10_cross_sell_opportunity.trigger: Customer expressing need that maps to a product not in current subscription
D10_cross_sell_opportunity.csm_language: You mentioned [need] — we actually have a solution for that: [product]. Based on what I know about your operation, it could [specific benefit].
D10_cross_sell_opportunity.csa_language: Based on what you shared about [need], I think [product] could really help. Let me introduce you to the right person to walk through it.
actions: Prepare warm handoff to Sales (if significant)
Include customer context and preferences in handoff

D11_product_issue_escalated.name: Product Issue Escalated
D11_product_issue_escalated.trigger: Customer experiencing product bug, integration failure, or feature malfunction
D11_product_issue_escalated.csm_language: I've escalated this to our product team as a priority. I'll personally track this and keep you updated until it's resolved.
D11_product_issue_escalated.csa_language: I've logged this with our technical team and I'm tracking it personally. Let me give you a timeline for next steps.
actions: Document issue with detail (steps to reproduce, screenshots)
Notify CS Manager if impacting Tier I account

D12_training_requested.name: Training Session Requested/Scheduled
D12_training_requested.trigger: Customer requests training, new staff hired, or utilization data suggests training gap
D12_training_requested.csm_language: Happy to arrange a refresher for your team. I'll tailor the session to focus on the areas that matter most for your operation.
D12_training_requested.csa_language: Let's get your team up to speed. I'll customize the training to focus on [specific features] based on your current usage.
actions: Identify training scope (new staff vs. refresher vs. new feature)
Follow up 2 weeks after to check adoption

D13_nps_promoter.name: NPS Promoter — Thank You
D13_nps_promoter.csm_language: Thank you for the positive feedback — it means a lot. Would you be open to sharing your experience as a case study or referral?
D13_nps_promoter.csa_language: Thank you so much! I'm glad to hear your team is getting great value. Would you be interested in being a reference for similar dealerships?
actions: Identify if they'd participate in case study

D14_nps_passive.name: NPS Passive — Follow-Up
D14_nps_passive.csm_language: Thank you for the feedback. I'd love to understand what would take your experience from good to great — any specific areas where we could improve?
D14_nps_passive.csa_language: Appreciate the feedback. I want to make sure you're getting the most out of everything available to you — can we schedule a quick check-in?
actions: Schedule call to identify improvement areas

D15_nps_detractor.name: NPS Detractor — Recovery
D15_nps_detractor.csm_language: Thank you for your honest feedback — I take this seriously and want to personally understand what's not working so we can fix it.
D15_nps_detractor.csa_language: I really appreciate you sharing this. I want to get on a call with you as soon as possible to understand what we need to do better.
actions: Listen first — understand root cause before offering solutions
Document root cause and plan in CI Dashboard
D15_nps_detractor.follow_up: 24 hours for initial contact, then weekly until resolved

steps: 1. Open CI Dashboard → Disposition Tracking tab
5. Add detailed notes (what happened, what was discussed, what was agreed)
7. Assign to self or appropriate team member
best_practices: Log dispositions IMMEDIATELY after the interaction — don't batch them
Be specific in notes — future you (or a colleague) needs to understand context
Always set a follow-up date — no open-ended dispositions
Use the urgency level honestly — everything can't be 'High'

_module_m4_tier_specific_knowledge.title: Tier-Specific Knowledge — Deep Dive
_module_m4_tier_specific_knowledge.description: Tier characteristics, engagement cadence per tier, upgrade/downgrade decision criteria, and tier-specific CS playbooks.

ci_dashboard_tiers.note: The CI Dashboard uses a runtime tier reclassification based on ARR: Tier I (≥$100K ARR), Tier II ($20K-$99K ARR), Tier III (<$20K ARR). These revenue tiers are independent of the product tiers (Protect/Enrich/Elevate/Control) but correlate with customer size and complexity.

tier_i.profile: Large dealer groups, multi-rooftop operations, enterprise accounts

engagement_cadence.ad_hoc: Available on demand — Tier I gets priority response

tier_i.escalation_sensitivity: ANY issue with a Tier I account escalates immediately to CS Manager. Health score entering amber = immediate review. Competitive threat = same-day CS Manager notification.
key_risks: Competitor targeting (700Credit, NCC actively pursuing large groups)

cs_playbook.relationship_depth: Know multiple stakeholders — DP, CFO, GSM, F&I Director. Don't let the relationship be single-threaded.
cs_playbook.value_documentation: Maintain running ROI document. Update quarterly with fraud prevented, compliance gaps closed, time saved, recontracts avoided.
cs_playbook.proactive_innovation: Share product roadmap updates. Invite to beta programs. Position as a strategic partner, not just a vendor.
cs_playbook.margin_management: Monitor monthly. Bureau costs at this volume can shift margin significantly. Proactive optimization, not reactive reprice.

tier_ii.profile: Mid-size dealer groups (3-10 rooftops), established franchise dealers
tier_ii.escalation_sensitivity: Health score entering red = CS Manager notification within 48 hours. Competitive threat = CS Manager notification within 1 week.
key_risks: Mid-value but higher churn probability than Tier I
Growing into Tier I OR contracting — monitor trajectory

cs_playbook.growth_focus: Identify Tier II accounts trending toward Tier I. Build expansion plan. These are your future strategic accounts.
cs_playbook.feature_utilization: Ensure they're using features commensurate with their tier. Under-utilized Tier II = churn risk.
cs_playbook.competitive_defense: Document differentiation before competitor approaches. Don't wait for the threat.
cs_playbook.margin_monitoring: Quarterly margin review. Bureau optimization proactive, not reactive.

tier_iii.profile: Single rooftop dealers, small independents, Foundation/Detect legacy, new customers in early lifecycle
tier_iii.escalation_sensitivity: Health score entering red = review within 1 week. Competitive threat = CS Manager notification within 2 weeks.
key_risks: Price-sensitive — reprices must be handled carefully
May not justify intensive CS investment
But: many Tier III accounts grow into Tier II

cs_playbook.efficiency_focus: Use scalable engagement — training webinars, self-service resources, automated health alerts. Reserve 1:1 time for at-risk accounts.
cs_playbook.adoption_critical: First 90 days determine retention. Focus on time-to-value (<14 days) and feature breadth adoption.
cs_playbook.upgrade_path: Identify Tier III accounts with growth potential. Position tier upgrade when utilization justifies it.
cs_playbook.margin_reality: Small accounts can have disproportionate margin issues. Flag negative margin quickly — these can't sustain long remediation periods.

protect.cs_focus: Compliance adoption, fraud prevention awareness, deal jacket usage
common_issues: Staff not using digital deal jackets for all deals
Red Flags checks being seen as friction
protect.upgrade_signal: Customer asking about SmartPencil, payment calculations, or wanting to speed up desk workflow → Enrich
protect.retention_lever: FTC compliance defense. If they leave Protect, they lose their compliance safety net.

enrich.cs_focus: SmartPencil adoption, payment accuracy, compliance + desk speed combination
common_issues: SmartPencil under-utilized — desk managers sticking to old tools
Score visibility frustration (Enrich doesn't show score)
Lender program matching not calibrated properly
enrich.upgrade_signal: Customer wants credit score visibility, soft pulls for pre-qualification, or credit spend controls → Elevate
enrich.retention_lever: SmartPencil desk speed + compliance combination. Show time-per-deal improvement and payment accuracy data.

elevate.cs_focus: Soft pull strategy, Credit Guardrails utilization, Customer Insights adoption, credit cost optimization
onboarding_priority: All Enrich setup + Soft pull configuration
Credit Guardrails setup and manager training
common_issues: Soft pull adoption slower than expected
Credit Guardrails seen as restrictive rather than informative
Customer Insights not being used for lead prioritization
elevate.upgrade_signal: Customer expanding to multiple rooftops, needs enterprise reporting, or wants full credit hard+soft control → Control
elevate.retention_lever: Cost savings from soft pull strategy. Calculate monthly savings from hard pull reduction.

control.cs_focus: Enterprise-level management, multi-rooftop consistency, full platform optimization
common_issues: Reporting not being used by group management
control.upgrade_signal: Already at top tier — focus on depth of adoption and expansion to new rooftops
control.retention_lever: Enterprise visibility and control that no competitor can match. Group-level compliance, fraud prevention, and credit management in one view.

upgrade_criteria.utilization_based: Customer consistently using 80%+ of current tier features for 3+ months
upgrade_criteria.need_based: Customer expressing need for features in higher tier
upgrade_criteria.growth_based: Customer adding rooftops, increasing deal volume, expanding operations
upgrade_criteria.margin_based: Tier upgrade would improve margin through better pricing alignment
upgrade_criteria.competitive_based: Tier upgrade closes feature gap that competitor is exploiting

downgrade_criteria.caution: Downgrades should be rare and always approved by CS Manager
valid_reasons: Customer consolidating operations and genuinely needs fewer features
Retention negotiation where downgrade is explicitly better than churn
Product no longer relevant due to customer's business model change
downgrade_criteria.process: Customer requests → CSA/CSM documents → CS Manager reviews → if approved, RevOps processes → monitor for 90 days post-downgrade
